reasoning: The base rate of the WHO declaring a new coronavirus pandemic in any given year is extremely low (sub 1%). While a new bat coronavirus capable of human transmission was reportedly discovered in a lab in February 2025, the mere discovery or cataloging of such viruses is relatively common in virology and does not at all guarantee an outbreak or pandemic. Several key steps must occur for a pandemic declaration: human spillover, sustained human-to-human transmission, global spread, and ultimately a formal WHO declaration, all within a 10-month window. Because these events are exceedingly rare, the probability is set to the minimum allowable value of 0.02.
